choice: Klebsiella pneumoniae first; S. aureus/MRSA documented follow-up
rationale: Depth over breadth per the challenge; rich BV-BRC lab-AST data and clear mechanisms.

choice: Self-source from BV-BRC; lab-measured AST only (evidence == 'Laboratory Method')
rationale: No organizer dataset; challenge warns against model-generated 'general phenotype' fields.

choice: AMRFinderPlus via pinned Docker image under WSL2
rationale: NCBI default; --organism Klebsiella_pneumoniae enables QRDR point mutations; Linux-native so Docker/WSL2.

choice: Per-antibiotic L2 logistic regression, class_weight='balanced' (classical ML is the star)
rationale: Fast, calibratable, explainable; per-drug models allow independent calibration/conformal/evidence.

choice: Sigmoid calibration (cv='prefit') + conformal prediction (crepes Mondrian primary, MAPIE cross-check)
rationale: Coverage-guaranteed abstention; sigmoid safer than isotonic for small per-drug folds.

choice: Homology-aware grouped split: MLST ST primary, Mash single-linkage @ ANI 99.5% fallback; StratifiedGroupKFold + unseen-lineage holdout
rationale: Prevents near-identical clonal genomes leaking across splits and inflating metrics.

choice: LLM surgical & structurally barred from prediction: evidence RAG, grounded report, LLM-as-reviewer; OpenAI primary, MockLLMClient in CI
rationale: Verdicts must be deterministic/auditable; LLM output schemas contain no verdict field; import-boundary CI check.

choice: Streamlit front + FastAPI backend; deploy to Streamlit Community Cloud
rationale: Challenge requires a Streamlit/Gradio demo; FastAPI gives a clean reusable API surface.

choice: Apache-2.0 + DISCLAIMER
rationale: Explicit patent grant, startup/acquisition-friendly for a patent-aware author; permissive for reuse.

choice: Full six-layer Sustainable Agentic SE framework as a live case study; documentation written before code
rationale: Ships fast while generating fresh ground-truth for the paper; no finding lives only in chat.

choice: arc42 as 12 chapter folders each with README.md (matching open-garden-planner); prd.md in chapter 01; verbatim challenge PDF as challenge-brief.md in chapter 01; all process flows as mermaid
rationale: Harness-hardening review by Sebastian — the initial six-layer setup must match the reference harness exactly before building on it.

choice: senior-reviewer agent (mandatory pre-PR gate) + skills (debug-verbose, finalize-epic, gf-build-and-run, gf-data-and-annotation); gitignore .claude/.mcp.json and Documentation/reuse-inventory.md
rationale: Replicate the open-garden-planner .claude harness; keep API keys (Tavily/OpenAI) and the private reuse map out of git.

choice: Draft-PR is the mandatory end state; PR stays draft with a falsifiable manual-testing checklist until the user explicitly confirms; fresh-worktree senior review re-run until no P0/P1; Plan Mode + alternative designs before any non-trivial change
rationale: Issue #35 transfer: automated green + review are necessary but not sufficient; manual testing is sovereign (repeatedly overturned reviewed/green work in the reference project).

choice: Single-source version at src/genome_firewall/__init__.py:__version__ (pyproject reads it via hatch dynamic); never create tags manually; release automation deferred with documented future trigger/bump policy
rationale: Issue #35 transfer: removes the two-file drift-bug class; nothing to release at EPIC-0, so document the deferral instead of building release.yml prematurely.

choice: Run CI on pushes to all branches AND on pull requests (concurrency cancels superseded runs)
rationale: Issue #35 transfer: feature branches get green/red feedback before the draft PR is opened; cancel-in-progress bounds cost.

choice: Add 8 gf-* process skills (change-control, validation-and-qa, docs-and-writing, architecture-contract, research-methodology, proof-and-analysis, failure-archaeology, pr-analysis) + a routing table in CLAUDE.md; adapt reference skills, do not copy Qt/CAD/garden/.ogp/i18n rules
rationale: Issue #35 transfer: give agents explicit routing for change control, evidence, docs, safety invariants, research, proof, historical failures, and PR analysis before EPIC 1 accelerates.

choice: Mandatory, no-exceptions capture of every non-obvious bug/lesson in the same session: canonical detailed log at Documentation/11-risks-and-technical-debt/ §11.4 + debug-verbose case study + CLAUDE.md Known-AI-pitfall line + pinning regression test; senior-reviewer treats a missing capture as P1
rationale: Match the reference project's strictness (must not be less strict than open-garden-planner); a lesson that lives only in chat is lost.

choice: Floor the ml extra with numba>=0.60 (skips ancient numba 0.53.1/llvmlite 0.36.0 that break on py3.11/3.12); add tests/test_smoke.py to satisfy the coverage gate honestly
rationale: Pre-existing dependency defect on main made uv sync --all-extras fail all CI jobs; the scaffold had zero tests so the 80% coverage gate could not pass. Both fixed without weakening any gate.

choice: Pure Python: ftplib.FTP_TLS for the FTPS flat file + per-genome .fna, stdlib urllib.request for the Solr Data API cross-check; no WSL2/p3-CLI dependency
rationale: Cross-platform, unit-testable, matches 'demo pure-Python, no bio-tools in CI'; avoids a heavy WSL2 toolchain for what is plain FTPS/HTTPS.

choice: Add pyarrow>=16 as a core dependency for pandas to_parquet/read_parquet
rationale: The PRD's Artifacts section already commits to Parquet for every processed table; pandas has no Parquet engine of its own, and EPIC 3 will be the first to read labels.parquet back.

choice: Trust-critical parse/filter/canonicalize/collapse-duplicate logic lives in predictor/dataset.py (pure, no network, mypy --strict, coverage-gated); all FTPS/Solr network I/O lives only in scripts/, outside those gates by design. Majority-vote SIR collapse on (genome_id, antibiotic); ties/conflicts dropped, never guessed at. 3-class SIR persisted (Intermediate kept); binary S-vs-R collapse deferred to EPIC 3.
rationale: Puts the one filter that most determines label integrity (evidence=='Laboratory Method', ADR-0001) under the strictest gates in the repo; keeps network flakiness out of the trust-critical path.

choice: Confirmed against the real Solr API (taxon_id=573): evidence has exactly 3 values in production -- 'Computational Method' (1,728,894 rows), 'Laboratory Method' (85,291 rows, exact match to the research doc), 'Computational Prediction' (0 rows). The evidence=='Laboratory Method' filter is correctly scoped -- no lab data hides under a different label.
rationale: Issue #12 explicitly requires enumerating all distinct evidence values before finalizing the filter, not assuming the research doc's sandboxed estimate; running it live closes that to-validate item with a hard number.

choice: feature_matrix.parquet (needs AMRFinderPlus) deferred to EPIC 2/#17; train/test splits (ADR-0005 homology-aware MLST+Mash grouping) deferred to EPIC 3/#18. labels.parquet ships split-agnostic; genome_metadata.parquet's nullable mlst_st + the manifest's mlst.missing_fraction give #18 the Mash-fallback scope up front.
rationale: Keeps EPIC 1 to data ingestion only, matching the module dependency graph (annotation and splitting both require inputs EPIC 1 produces); carry-forward comments posted on #11/#13/#17/#18 so nothing is silently dropped.
